Enum Class Intensity

java.lang.Object
java.lang.Enum<Intensity>
hr.unizd.workoutlog.model.Intensity
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, Comparable<Intensity>, Constable
public enum Intensity extends Enum<Intensity>
Represents the intensity level of a workout.

Using an enum instead of a plain String ensures that only valid intensity values can be used throughout the application (type safety). Each constant carries a human-readable display name used in the user interface.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested classes/interfaces inherited from class Enum

    Enum.EnumDesc<E>

  • Enum Constant Summary

    Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    HARD
    High-effort workout requiring maximum exertion.
    LIGHT
    Low-effort workout, suitable for warm-up or recovery sessions.
    MEDIUM
    Moderate-effort workout, the most common training level.

  • Enum Constant Details

    • LIGHT

      public static final Intensity LIGHT
      Low-effort workout, suitable for warm-up or recovery sessions.

    • MEDIUM

      public static final Intensity MEDIUM
      Moderate-effort workout, the most common training level.

    • HARD

      public static final Intensity HARD
      High-effort workout requiring maximum exertion.

  • Field Details

    • displayName

      private final String displayName

  • Constructor Details

    • Intensity

      private Intensity(String displayName)
      Creates an Intensity constant with the given display name.

      Enum constructors are always private — Java enforces this automatically. The constructor runs once per constant when the class is first loaded.

      Parameters:
      displayName - human-readable label for this intensity level

  • Method Details

    • values

      public static Intensity[] values()
      Returns an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared.
      Returns:
      an array containing the constants of this enum class, in the order they are declared

    • valueOf

      public static Intensity valueOf(String name)
      Returns the enum constant of this class with the specified name. The string must match exactly an identifier used to declare an enum constant in this class. (Extraneous whitespace characters are not permitted.)
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the enum constant to be returned.
      Returns:
      the enum constant with the specified name
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if this enum class has no constant with the specified name
      NullPointerException - if the argument is null

    • getDisplayName

      public String getDisplayName()
      Returns the human-readable name of this intensity level.

      Used by the GUI to label radio buttons and by Workout.toString() to produce readable output in the workout list.

      Returns:
      display name (e.g. "Light", "Medium", "Hard")

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns the display name, so that an Intensity value can be placed directly into a JComboBox or printed without extra code.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Enum<Intensity>
      Returns:
      same as getDisplayName()
